 {"id":"bd-1uy.1.5","title":"Hostcall ABI: Consolidate Duplicate HostCallPayload/HostResultPayload Types","description":"# Goal
Eliminate (or strictly contain) duplicated hostcall ABI type definitions so the ABI cannot drift:
- `crate::extensions::{HostCallPayload, HostResultPayload, HostStreamChunk, ...}`
- `crate::connectors::{HostCallPayload, HostResultPayload, HostStreamChunk, ...}`

# Why This Is Not Cosmetic
Right now, the two type graphs are **not identical** (real drift already exists):
- `extensions::HostStreamChunk` includes `backpressure` fields; `connectors::HostStreamChunk` does not.
- error helpers in `connectors` produce `output: null` on errors, while protocol validation requires `output` is an object.

If we keep two competing definitions, we will eventually ship a protocol/schema mismatch.

# Scope
1. Choose a single canonical home for the ABI structs/enums.
   - Recommended: make `extensions::*` canonical, since it is tied directly to `ExtensionMessage` protocol/schema.

2. Make the non-canonical module:
   - re-export the canonical types, OR
   - become a thin newtype wrapper with explicit conversions.

3. Fix helper constructors to be spec-compliant everywhere:
   - `host_result_err*` must set `output: {}` (object), not `null`.
   - Ensure `error` presence matches `is_error`.

4. Update `HttpConnector` and any other connector code to use canonical types.

# Tests
- Compile-time:
  - ensure only one canonical type is used across modules (no parallel structs).
- Runtime:
  - round-trip serialize/deserialize between connector boundary and protocol boundary.
  - assert that any `HostResultPayload` produced by helpers passes `validate_host_result()`.
- Drift sentinel:
  - a unit test that asserts the JSON Schema (`docs/schema/extension_protocol.json`) matches the Rust type serialization shape (including backpressure fields).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] There is exactly one source-of-truth for hostcall ABI structs/enums.
- [ ] All helper constructors return schema-valid payloads.
- [ ] All call sites compile and tests pass.
- [ ] A drift sentinel test would fail if future fields are added to one side but not the other.","notes":"2026-02-06 HazyLynx: Consolidated connectors hostcall ABI types to canonical extensions types via re-export in src/connectors/mod.rs; added canonical-type compile sentinel + schema drift sentinel tests; validations: cargo test --lib connectors::tests (7 passed), cargo check --all-targets passed, cargo test --lib sse::tests passed. Drift sentinel test connectors_hostcall_types_are_canonical_extension_types passes. No duplicate struct definitions found anywhere.","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:31:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1uy.2","title":"Auth: Refresh expired OAuth tokens for extension providers (startup + deterministic logs)","description":"# Goal
Ensure extension-registered OAuth providers behave correctly in real runs by wiring automatic refresh for **expired extension OAuth tokens**.

# Background (Observed)
`src/auth.rs` implements `AuthStorage::refresh_expired_extension_oauth_tokens(...)` with unit tests, but there is no call site in the main runtime.

Current behavior risk:
- extension provider tokens can silently expire and never refresh unless the user re-auths
- conformance/E2E that rely on extension providers can become flaky or fail unexpectedly

# Scope
1) **Define refresh policy (spec-level)
- When to refresh:
  - on startup before running extensions
  - optionally just-in-time before first use of an extension provider
- What to refresh:
  - only expired tokens
  - skip built-in providers handled by `refresh_expired_oauth_tokens_with_client` (e.g. anthropic)
- Failure handling:
  - network failure: keep old token, emit actionable error/log event
  - invalid refresh token: mark token invalid and require re-login

2) **Wire call sites**
- Ensure extension configs (oauth provider configs) are available at the time refresh runs.
- Call refresh in all relevant entrypoints:
  - interactive mode
  - non-interactive (print)
  - RPC mode
- Ensure refresh respects:
  - timeouts
  - redaction policy (never log secrets)
  - deterministic structured logs for harness diffing

3) **Logging / Artifacts**
- Emit `pi.auth.refresh` events to the unified logging sink with:
  - provider id
  - token state transition (redacted)
  - timing
  - outcome classification
- Ensure bd-4u9 artifact index + redaction summary are used by any E2E script.

# Tests
- Unit tests: already exist for the refresh function; add any missing edge cases.
- Integration test:
  - execute a minimal runtime path that triggers the refresh call and asserts auth.json mutation
  - use VCR playback (no live network)
- E2E scenario:
  - drive a run where an extension provider token is expired and is refreshed automatically
  - capture deterministic JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9

## Acceptance Criteria
- Expired extension OAuth tokens refresh automatically without manual user intervention.
- Refresh behavior is deterministic under test harness control.
- No secrets leak into logs/artifacts.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Refresh policy is explicitly defined (startup vs JIT) and implemented
[ ] Refresh is wired for all modes (interactive, print, RPC)
[ ] No secrets leak into logs; redaction summary present for E2E artifacts
[ ] Integration/E2E coverage proves the refresh call site executes (VCR playback; no live network)
[ ] JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9 where applicable
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:02:21.387414725Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T10:02:53.786405649Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T10:02:53.786318427Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["auth","extensions","oauth","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uy.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:02:21.387414725Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":535,"issue_id":"bd-1uy.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed: Wired extension OAuth token refresh into main startup path.

Changes:
1. **src/main.rs** (~lines 369-401): After extension model entries are computed, builds a HashMap<String, OAuthConfig> from entries with oauth_config, then calls auth.refresh_expired_extension_oauth_tokens() with graceful error handling (warns on failure, continues startup).

2. **src/auth.rs** (~lines 282-314): Added structured tracing to refresh_expired_extension_oauth_tokens():
   - pi.auth.extension_oauth_refresh.start (info, with count)
   - pi.auth.extension_oauth_refresh.ok (info, per-provider, with elapsed_ms)
   - pi.auth.extension_oauth_refresh.error (warn, per-provider, with elapsed_ms)

3. **tests/extensions_provider_oauth.rs**: Added 5 new integration tests:
   - oauth_configs_from_entries_empty_when_no_oauth
   - oauth_configs_from_entries_extracts_providers_with_oauth
   - full_wiring_refresh_expired_token_via_mock_server (uses mock HTTP server)
   - full_wiring_no_refresh_when_token_valid
   - full_wiring_refresh_skips_providers_without_config

All 38 tests pass (20 existing + 5 new + 13 common). Lib compiles clean.","created_at":"2026-02-06T10:02:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1v10","title":"Phase 0: Environment and Runtime Setup","description":"# Phase 0: Environment and Runtime Setup

## Purpose
Before we can do ANY differential testing, we need BOTH runtimes working and verified independently. This phase ensures:
1. The pi-mono TypeScript runtime can load and run extensions via Bun
2. The Rust QuickJS runtime can load and run extensions
3. A deterministic test environment exists (no randomness, fixed timestamps)

## Why This Is Phase 0
Everything else depends on this. If either runtime cannot load extensions, differential testing is impossible. We discovered Bun 1.3.8 is available at /home/ubuntu/.bun/bin/bun. The pi-mono source lives at legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/. We need to verify it builds and its extension loader works.

## Acceptance Criteria
- pi-mono extension loader.ts can load the hello.ts extension and produce output
- Rust QuickJS runtime can load the hello.ts extension and produce output
- Both outputs can be captured as structured JSON
- Deterministic mode eliminates timestamps, random values, cwd-dependent paths","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:14:07.671337047Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:36:15.918993495Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:36:15.918905561Z","close_reason":"Phase 0 complete: pi-mono loads extensions via Bun/jiti (tests/ext_conformance/ts_oracle/load_extension.ts), Rust QuickJS runtime loads extensions (tests/ext_conformance_diff.rs), both produce structured JSON, all 60 official extensions validated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1v10","depends_on_id":"bd-3odv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:14:07.724213001Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":309,"issue_id":"bd-1v10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"KEY DISCOVERY: Bun 1.3.8 is available at /home/ubuntu/.bun/bin/bun, which also provides a node shim. pi-mono uses jiti for extension loading (not native Bun module resolution). The virtualModules mechanism in loader.ts maps package names to bundled versions (@sinclair/typebox, @mariozechner/pi-agent-core, etc.). In development mode it uses aliases instead. For our harness, we need to run in development mode (not Bun binary mode) so that jiti resolves imports via filesystem.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:27:14Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ve4","title":"CI: add cross-platform build/test matrix (linux/macOS/windows)","description":"# Goal
Increase release confidence by running CI on all target platforms (at minimum build + unit tests), and document any platform-specific exclusions.

# Background
The current CI workflow only runs on `ubuntu-latest`. That’s good for fast iteration but insufficient for shipping binaries.

# Scope
- Add a GitHub Actions matrix over:
  - `ubuntu-latest`
  - `macos-latest`
  - `windows-latest`
- For each OS:
  - `cargo fmt --check`
  - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
  - `cargo test --all-targets`

# Platform Considerations
- System deps:
  - `fd` and `rg` are required/optional for tool tests; install equivalents per OS.
- Path dependencies:
  - CI currently clones sibling repos (asupersync/rich_rust/charmed_rust/sqlmodel_rust). Ensure the same approach works on macOS/Windows.
- VCR playback mode:
  - Keep `VCR_MODE=playback` for determinism.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ci.yml` runs on Linux/macOS/Windows.
- [ ] Any OS-specific skips are explicit and justified (no silent test omissions).
- [ ] Failures include enough logs to debug platform-specific issues.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:21:48.739486913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:27:15.998454113Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T00:29:34.304318188Z","close_reason":"Cross-platform CI matrix in .github/workflows/ci.yml (commit 292102c)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ve4","depends_on_id":"bd-gqtd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1vfi","title":"E2E infra: discover runnable providers from models/auth/env at runtime","description":"Implement provider/key discovery used by live E2E tests so the suite automatically tests every provider that is actually configured.

Scope:
- Build deterministic discovery logic that merges:
  1) ~/.pi/agent/models.json (or configured model registry path)
  2) auth store entries (api_key/oauth)
  3) environment variables
- Produce a normalized provider matrix (provider id, model id, auth source, required config fields, enabled/disabled reason).
- Ensure discovery supports OpenAI-compatible providers (OpenRouter, xAI, DeepSeek, etc.) in addition to first-party providers.
- Emit discovery report artifact (JSON + markdown summary) before test execution.

Requirements:
- Redact secrets in logs/artifacts while preserving enough context for debugging.
- If models.json path is missing or malformed, tests should fail with actionable diagnostics (not silent skip).
- Discovery output must be the single source of truth for provider test selection.
